title: Daily Market Digest: July 20, 2026
subtitle: Market navigating geopolitical tensions and key earnings reports

items.headline: U.S. Stocks Exhibit Mixed Performance Amid Geopolitical Headwinds and Earnings Season Kick-off
items.summary: U.S. stock markets are showing a mixed performance today as investors weigh escalating U.S.-Iran tensions against a busy week of technology earnings reports. While certain tech and AI-related stocks saw early gains, broader sentiment remains cautious after last week's sell-off in megacap technology and semiconductor shares. Attention is shifting towards central bank expectations and upcoming corporate earnings to gauge market direction. This is not financial advice.

items.headline: AMC Soars on Strong Q2 Earnings
items.summary: AMC Entertainment Holdings ($AMC) shares jumped 11% after the movie theater operator reported record Q2 revenue and adjusted EBITDA, surpassing Wall Street's expectations amid a strong box office environment.

items.headline: AMD Climbs on Microsoft Partnership
items.summary: Advanced Micro Devices ($AMD) shares climbed 4.5% in premarket trading after Microsoft ($MSFT) announced it would use AMD's Helios system in its data centers, expanding their AI data center partnership.

items.headline: Hut 8 Up on AI Data Center Expansion
items.summary: Hut 8 ($HUT) gained 16% as the company fully commercialized the second phase of its Beacon Point AI data center campus, securing a significant lease and doubling contracted capacity.

items.headline: Globavend Holdings Plummets
items.summary: Globavend Holdings Limited ($GVH) experienced a sharp decline of 59.66% today.

items.headline: Oracle Falls in Early Trading
items.summary: Oracle ($ORCL) fell 4.4% in early trading, with the stock continuing to face downward pressure.

items.headline: ServiceNow Slips
items.summary: ServiceNow ($NOW) saw its shares slip 4.2% in early market movements.

items.headline: Technology and Energy Sectors See Divergent Trends
items.summary: The Technology sector showed signs of rebounding today, leading among major indices in early trading. This follows a volatile previous week where a sharp sell-off in AI-related stocks and semiconductors weighed heavily on the sector. Meanwhile, Energy continued to be a notable performer, having advanced last week, buoyed by rising oil prices amidst geopolitical instability. Communication Services and Consumer Discretionary also experienced declines last Friday.

items.headline: Leading Economic Index Declines as Fed Holds Closed Meeting
items.summary: The Conference Board Leading Economic Index (LEI) for the U.S. declined by 0.2% in June 2026, partially reversing gains from prior months. While consumer spending is weakening, strong business investment related to AI is expected to support economic activity as inflation continues to improve. In central bank news, the Federal Reserve Board is holding a pre-scheduled closed meeting today at 3:30 p.m. to review and determine advance and discount rates. The Fed is currently in a blackout period ahead of its FOMC decision.

items.headline: Earnings Season Gathers Pace with Major Tech Reports Ahead
items.summary: Earnings season is picking up significantly this week, with highly anticipated reports expected from major technology companies including Tesla ($TSLA), Alphabet ($GOOGL), Intel ($INTC), and IBM ($IBM). This follows recent Q2 earnings announcements from companies such as AMC Entertainment ($AMC) and Domino's Pizza ($DPZ). Investors will be keenly watching these reports for insights into corporate performance and future outlook.

items.headline: U.S.-Iran Tensions Continue to Influence Market Sentiment
items.summary: Geopolitical tensions involving the U.S. and Iran remain a significant factor impacting market sentiment. Fresh U.S. airstrikes on Iran continued to keep geopolitical risks in focus, influencing oil prices and fostering a cautious mood among investors. These developments contribute to a broader environment where markets are likely to remain highly headline-driven.
